_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_0: N/A — anti-gaming guard, derived from the harbor benchmark's defensive floor (no positive weight, can only zero the trial). Not a user ask.
_provenance.goal_1: The whole session is rooted in Turn 0 ('i dont understand how this can happen at all') over a merge that made image detection capability-gated and thus broke it; Turn 4 ('can't we do startsWith to short circuit?') and Turn 6 ('optimize the includes() check') confirm the surviving behavior is a substring/includes-style check over the raw escape sequences, not a capability lookup. This is the central correctness restoration.
_provenance.goal_2: Turn 1 ('there was a pr merge from a user called can, guess we need to revert that'), Turn 2 ('yes'). The user explicitly directed a full revert of the merge. Behaviorally this means the added helper and its import/usage sites must be unwound, not just shadowed — described without naming the specific symbol/file the merge introduced.
_provenance.goal_3: Turns 1-2 directed reverting the entire merge, which (per the model patch and F2P gates f2p_box_cache_reverted / f2p_markdown_wraps_unconditionally) also touched the box cache structure and the markdown wrap loop. A complete revert must undo these collateral changes, expressed behaviorally rather than by naming the RenderCache type.
_provenance.goal_4: Turn 3 ('then do a patch release') is an explicit instruction to release after the revert, and Turn 0 establishes that a changelog entry is required before merge and sets the changelog format rules — so a proper patch release bundles the version bump with a matching changelog entry. Kept lower-weighted than the code restoration because a version bump alone (the reward-0.2 model patch) is worthless without the revert it accompanies.

_dropped_from_v2.note: No v2 rubric existed on main for this task. This is the first-pass rubric authored at the v3 bar via the scaleout author↔critic loop.

_added_for_coverage.note: Not applicable — no v2 baseline to diff against. Per-goal user-ask tracing lives in each goal's rationale and in _provenance.

Gemini critic final: verdict=REVISE score=0.875 round=3.
strength: Excellent behavioral framing in goal_1 that captures the required string-check logic without mandating specific keywords or method names.
strength: Strong anti-gaming floor (goal_0) that specifically targets the lockfile-only version bump seen in the model patches.
strength: Clear and comprehensive coverage of the collateral reverts (goal_3) without over-specifying the exact type names.
residual risk: Turn-number citation mismatches in goal_1's rationale and the decomposition notes could confuse an evaluator trying to trace the user's intent, as Turn 7 actually says 'probably not'.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine source-code change that actually reverts the bad merge and restores correct image-detection behavior, not a gaming artifact. Reject if the diff is empty, only edits a lockfile/version string without any logic change, only adds comments or whitespace, or merely reorders code without changing behavior. Reject non-code artifacts such as a captured directory-tree banner whose entire content is a '=== repo/ ===' style header. Reject git-ops exfiltration that fakes work instead of producing real source changes (e.g. 'git checkout main' or 'git reset --hard origin/...' left as the only change). Reject solutions that delete or weaken the relevant tests instead of fixing the code, hardcode a value purely to satisfy one assertion, or confine changes to temporary/scratch/unmerged locations rather than the real source tree.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming floor calibrated against the lowest-reward model patch (reward 0.2), whose ENTIRE diff was a single package-lock.json version bump (0.71.0 -> 0.71.1) with no source change — a version-only / lockfile-only artifact that must be flagged as not a genuine revert. The accepted change touches real detection logic across multiple source files, so version-only, comment-only, or git-ops-only diffs are gaming.

completeness_goals.goal: Image-line detection is restored to a pure, unconditional string check: the code decides whether a line contains an inline-image escape sequence purely by inspecting the line's string content for the terminal image-protocol markers, with NO dependency on terminal-capability detection or a capability/escape-prefix lookup. A line carrying a Kitty graphics sequence or an iTerm2 inline-image sequence must be detected as an image, including when the sequence is preceded by leading whitespace/indentation or other content on the same line, while ordinary text (and strings that merely resemble a path or filename) must NOT be detected. The check must be exercised by real runtime logic on actual strings; a stub that always returns the same value, a capability-gated check, a hardcoded result, or a parsed-but-ignored implementation does not satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: The whole session is rooted in Turn 0 ('i dont understand how this can happen at all') over a merge that made image detection capability-gated and thus broke it; Turn 4 ('can't we do startsWith to short circuit?') and Turn 6 ('optimize the includes() check') confirm the surviving behavior is a substring/includes-style check over the raw escape sequences, not a capability lookup. This is the central correctness restoration.

completeness_goals.goal: The bad merge is actually reverted at the source level, not merely patched around: the capability-gated image-detection helper that the merge added to the terminal-image module is removed (no longer exported from that module), and the consumer modules that the merge wired to import and special-case it no longer reference that removed helper — they import nothing that was deleted and no longer call the removed helper (they may still legitimately branch on whether a line is an image using the restored capability-independent detection). After the revert, every touched source file still parses and the package still builds; leaving the dead helper exported, or leaving stale imports/branches that reference removed symbols, does not satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 1 ('there was a pr merge from a user called can, guess we need to revert that'), Turn 2 ('yes'). The user explicitly directed a full revert of the merge. Behaviorally this means the added helper and its import/usage sites must be unwound, not just shadowed — described without naming the specific symbol/file the merge introduced.

completeness_goals.goal: Collateral refactors introduced by the same bad merge are reverted so the affected components return to their prior, simpler shape and behavior. Specifically, the render/output components that the merge changed must no longer carry the merge's new abstractions or conditional image-special-casing that the revert undoes: the component that gained a new consolidated cache abstraction returns to caching via its prior plain fields with equivalent cache-validity behavior, and the markdown rendering path no longer special-cases image lines while wrapping but wraps its lines along the prior unconditional path. Equivalent behavior, not a specific identifier or type name, is what matters; merely renaming symbols while preserving the merge's structure, or leaving the special-casing in place, does not satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turns 1-2 directed reverting the entire merge, which (per the model patch and F2P gates f2p_box_cache_reverted / f2p_markdown_wraps_unconditionally) also touched the box cache structure and the markdown wrap loop. A complete revert must undo these collateral changes, expressed behaviorally rather than by naming the RenderCache type.

completeness_goals.goal: A patch-level release is cut for the affected package as part of completing the revert: the package's published version is incremented by a patch step (only the lowest version component advances, no minor/major bump) and the bump is reflected consistently wherever the package version is recorded so the project is releasable, AND a corresponding changelog entry documenting the revert is added to the affected package's changelog under the appropriate section, following the project's established changelog format. A version bump on the wrong package, a non-patch bump, an inconsistent/partial bump, a release that omits the changelog entry, or a version edit unaccompanied by the actual code revert does not satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 3 ('then do a patch release') is an explicit instruction to release after the revert, and Turn 0 establishes that a changelog entry is required before merge and sets the changelog format rules — so a proper patch release bundles the version bump with a matching changelog entry. Kept lower-weighted than the code restoration because a version bump alone (the reward-0.2 model patch) is worthless without the revert it accompanies.

decomposition_notes: Evidence tier is model_only (golden patch empty; 3 model patches, one scored 0.2). The instruction.md Turn-0 ask is a generic 'review this PR' template, but the substantive deliverable emerges across the multi-turn session: revert a bad merge from user 'can' that made inline-image detection capability-gated (breaking it), and cut a patch release. goal_1 (highest weight) captures restoring image detection to a pure, capability-independent substring check that handles Kitty + iTerm2 sequences, indentation, and rejects plain/look-alike text — the heart of the F2P containsImage behavior/purity gates and the optimization discussion in Turns 4-9. goal_2 captures the explicit Turn 1-2 directive to actually revert the merge at the source (remove the added helper, unwind its imports/usages). goal_3 captures the collateral reverts to the box cache and markdown wrap loop that the same merge introduced (F2P gates f2p_box_cache_reverted, f2p_markdown_wraps_unconditionally), expressed behaviorally. goal_4 captures the explicit Turn 3 'then do a patch release'. The later turns (4-8: startsWith short-circuit, single-row vs multi-row images, VT code sequences, 'probably not', 'you just proposed') are an exploratory optimization discussion the user ultimately declines ('probably not') and where the user notes the agent only proposed rather than applied a change — so no NEW code deliverable is owed beyond the restored includes()-style check already covered by goal_1; these turns are intentionally not scored as separate deliverables. The anti-gaming floor (goal_0) is calibrated against the reward-0.2 patch, which was a lockfile-only version bump with zero source change. Weights deliberately differ from the F2P manifest (which front-loads the two upstream runtime gates at 0.2 each and the containsImage behavior at 0.15); here user-importance ranks the behavioral detection restoration (goal_1) and the explicit revert directive (goal_2) highest, with the collateral reverts and the patch release weighted lower. Sum of weighted goals = 0.42 + 0.30 + 0.18 + 0.10 = 1.00.
